focus: RQ5 groundwork: source-backed Adopt/Adapt/Reject/Defer matrix for GitNexus patterns across Public owners.

graphEvents.label: Code Graph phase and change-safety packet
Code Graph confidence and explanation metadata packet

label: Adopt GitNexus phase-DAG hygiene for Code Graph scan/readiness stages
Adapt GitNexus single graph accumulator as bounded scan-stage state, not persistent truth
Reject a unified Public graph across code, memory, skills, routes, tools, and embeddings
Adopt confidence-bearing edge presentation because Public already stores confidence/provenance/evidence metadata
Adapt reason/step-style explanation metadata per owner without importing code relation semantics into Memory
Adapt impact and detect_changes as read-only Code Graph safety surfaces gated by readiness
Reject mutating rename; adapt only an advisory preview shape
Defer route, shape, api_impact, and group contracts until Public owns route-consumer or multi-repo contract facts
Adopt GitNexus tool/resource affordances as Skill Advisor derived and graph-causal routing evidence
Adapt freshness display independently per owner rather than sharing one freshness enum across systems

direction: Use GitNexus as a shared graph substrate for all Public graph-like systems
reason: Owner truth units differ: Code Graph structural code edges, Memory causal memory lineage, Skill Advisor skill routing metadata.

direction: Expose mutating rename in Public near-term
reason: GitNexus preview/apply semantics can diverge; Public should remain advisory until exact range application is proven.

label: The matrix supports five follow-up owner lanes: Code Graph phase/change-safety, Code Graph metadata/explanations, Skill Advisor affordance evidence, Memory trust display, and deferred multi-repo contracts.
